skill.name: basecamp-automation
skill.description: Automates Basecamp projects, to-do lists, tasks, messages, people, and access workflows through the Rube MCP Basecamp toolkit.
skill.summary: Manage Basecamp projects, tasks, messages, people, and access through Rube MCP workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but these are false positives from markdown inline code, HTML examples, and Basecamp tool names in SKILL.md. The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated: the skill requires an external Rube MCP endpoint and can perform mutating Basecamp operations, including tasks, messages, and project access changes.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Service Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This is a legitimate integration endpoint, but it routes Basecamp automation through an external service and requires users to trust that service with connected account actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is explicitly documented as part of setup, and the skill depends on Rube MCP. This is a clear network trust boundary, not evidence of malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Basecamp Write and Access Modification Capabilities
medium_findings.description: The workflow documents tools that create to-do lists, create tasks, post messages, update messages, and grant or revoke project access. These are expected capabilities, but mistakes or unauthorized prompts could alter project data or membership.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed Basecamp tools and parameters directly describe mutating actions. The behavior is intentional for this productivity skill, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many markdown backtick spans as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md contains documentation for MCP tool names and parameters, not executable Ruby, shell scripts, or command invocations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only scanned file is markdown, and the flagged text is inline documentation for Basecamp and Rube MCP tools. No executable code path exists in the skill package.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts correspond to ordinary words and markdown content such as description fields, HTML formatting notes, and Basecamp message terminology. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose and parameter descriptions at the cited locations, not cryptographic functions or algorithms. The scanner appears to have matched substrings in documentation.

low_findings.title: Project and People Enumeration Is Intended Functionality
low_findings.description: The skill lists projects, to-do lists, and people to resolve Basecamp IDs before actions. This can expose workspace metadata to the assistant context, but it is necessary for the documented workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly instructs use of listing tools for ID resolution. This is legitimate Basecamp automation behavior, but it carries privacy and least-privilege considerations.

content.value_statement: Basecamp work often requires repetitive project, task, message, and people management steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for safe Basecamp automation through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Find Basecamp projects and resolve project, bucket, to-do set, message board, and person IDs.
Create Basecamp to-do lists and to-do items with assignees, due dates, and HTML descriptions.
Post and update Basecamp message board messages using the correct project and board IDs.
List people and project members before granting, revoking, or creating project access.
Create and inspect color-coded to-do groups within existing Basecamp to-do lists.
Guide pagination, status filters, ID formats, and common Basecamp API pitfalls.
limitations: Requires a connected Rube MCP server and an active Basecamp OAuth connection.
Does not execute Basecamp actions without the required MCP tools being available.
Rich text fields must use HTML formatting rather than Markdown.
Write operations can change live Basecamp data, so users should confirm targets before changes.

use_cases.title: Create Project Task Plans
use_cases.description: Turn a project plan into Basecamp to-do lists, tasks, assignees, and due dates.

use_cases.title: Publish Team Updates
use_cases.description: Post structured status updates to the correct Basecamp project message board.

use_cases.title: Manage Project Membership
use_cases.description: Review project people and prepare access changes for collaborators or clients.

prompt_templates.title: List My Projects
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Basecamp automation skill to list my active Basecamp projects. Show project names and user-facing links when available.

prompt_templates.title: Create A To-Do List
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Basecamp project named [project name]. Create a to-do list named [list name] with these tasks: [tasks]. Confirm the project and list before creating anything.

prompt_templates.title: Post A Project Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Post a Basecamp message in [project name] with subject [subject]. Use this update text: [message]. Format the body as simple HTML and return the app URL.

prompt_templates.title: Audit And Organize Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the to-do lists in [project name], identify duplicate or stale lists, propose a cleanup plan, and ask for approval before creating groups or changing access.

output_examples.input: Create a launch checklist in the Website Redesign project.
output_examples.output: Found the Website Redesign project and its to-do set. Created the Launch Checklist list with tasks for QA, analytics, content approval, and release notes. Returned the Basecamp app link for review.

output_examples.input: Post today's status update to the client project.
output_examples.output: Resolved the project and message board, formatted the update as HTML, published it as an active message, and provided the user-facing Basecamp link.

output_examples.input: Add Morgan to the client onboarding project if they are not already there.
output_examples.output: Checked all people, matched Morgan by name, reviewed current project members, and prepared the access grant for confirmation before changing membership.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so the current Basecamp schemas are used.
Resolve names to IDs before any write or access change operation.
Ask for confirmation before posting messages, creating tasks, or changing project access.
anti_patterns: Do not reconstruct Basecamp URLs manually from IDs.
Do not use Markdown in Basecamp rich text fields that require HTML.
Do not create duplicate to-do lists without checking existing lists first.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Basecamp account?
faq.answer: Yes. You need an active Basecamp connection through Rube MCP before workflows can run.

faq.question: Can it create and assign Basecamp tasks?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents workflows for creating to-dos with assignees, due dates, descriptions, and notifications.

faq.question: Can it post Basecamp messages?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide message creation and updates through the correct project message board tools.

faq.question: Can it manage project access?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows to list people and grant, revoke, or create project users.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it uses an external MCP service and can change live Basecamp project data.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The package contains markdown instructions for MCP tool usage, not scripts or executable source code.
